text: Day 48 roundup: Trump 'war very close to over' — Round 2 talks 'next two days.' Ceasefire extension 'in principle' 2 weeks. Senate rejects war powers 47-52. Munir in Tehran. Lavrov-Xi energy pact. Iran recovering missile launchers at Khomein. Security cabinet no Lebanon ceasefire. Hegseth impeachment 6 articles. Sanctions waiver expires April 19. Brent $94.66. Gas $4.108. S&P 7,020 record.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNN, Fortune, Roll Call, Bloomberg, CNBC, Al Jazeera, Jerusalem Post, Axios, AAA.

text: SATELLITE INTEL: Iran clearing debris at Khomein underground missile base. Heavy equipment (front-end loaders, excavators, dump trucks) at tunnel entrances. US intel: ~50% of Iran's missile launchers remain intact — buried but not destroyed. Iran using ceasefire window to reconstitute capability. This is a significant ceasefire compliance concern.
verificationNote: TheDefenseNews and IBTimes confirmed satellite imagery. US intelligence estimate cited by multiple outlets.

text: HORMUZ BLOCKADE DAY 3: CENTCOM says Iran sea trade 'completely halted.' 9 vessels turned back in 48h. USS Spruance redirected Iranian-flagged cargo. Rich Starry tanker (sanctioned) attempted breach — turned back. Only 4 vessels transited near Iran (Greek Agios Fanourios, Chinese Alicia/RHN, Iranian Golbon). 2,800+ ships stranded; 40,000+ seafarers affected.
verificationNote: CENTCOM press releases, Washington Post, CNBC confirmed details.

text: BINT JBEIL: IDF 98th Division (Paratroopers, Commando, Givati) nearing full capture. 100+ Hezbollah killed including Radwan Force. April 15: 16-20 killed across south Lebanon — triple-tap paramedic strike in Mayfadoun, family killed in Jbaa. Netanyahu released video DURING security cabinet meeting vowing strikes continue. Cabinet ended with NO decision on Lebanon ceasefire.
verificationNote: JPost, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel confirmed operations. Jerusalem Post confirmed cabinet meeting.

text: DIPLOMACY TRACKER: (1) Ceasefire extension 'in principle' — 2 more weeks past April 22 but US hasn't formally committed. (2) Pakistan FM Munir in Tehran with US message for Round 2. (3) Israel-Lebanon first direct talks since 1993 at State Dept. (4) Lavrov-Xi summit: Russia offers energy lifeline. (5) 60-day War Powers deadline April 29. (6) Sanctions waiver expires April 19 — NOT renewed.
verificationNote: Fortune/AP, Bloomberg, NPR, CNBC, Time confirmed all items.

text: MARKETS DAY 48: Oil softens — Brent $94.66 (-2.2%), WTI $90.72 on Round 2 talk hopes. S&P 7,020 new record — erased ALL war losses. Gold pulls back to $4,825 (from $5,000+). DXY ~98.05 (6-week low). FERTILIZER CRISIS: urea $826/ton (+74%), ammonia topped $1,000/ton. USDA: 3% less corn planted. IMF: 3.1% growth, recession warning. Consumer sentiment record low 47.6. March CPI 3.3%.
verificationNote: Trading Economics, AAA, CME, IMF WEO April 2026, EIA STEO confirmed.
